problem.statement: Given a binary tree, print its **zigzag level-order traversal**: one line per depth level, values space-separated; level 0 reads left-to-right, level 1 right-to-left, level 2 left-to-right, and so on, alternating every level.
Given a binary tree, print the **diameter** — the length (in edges) of the longest path between any two nodes.
Given strings `s` and `p`, print all **starting indices** (ascending, space-separated) in `s` where a permutation (anagram) of `p` occurs. Print an empty line if there are none.
Given strings `s1`, `s2`, `s3`, print `true` if `s3` is formed by an **interleaving** of `s1` and `s2` (preserving each string's relative character order), else `false`.
Given a **binary search tree** and two values `p`, `q` known to exist in it, print the value of their **lowest common ancestor**.

Given two sorted integer arrays `nums1` (length `m`) and `nums2` (length `n`), print the **median** of the combined sorted array without fully materializing and sorting the merge (aim for O(log(min(m, n))) time). Print the median as a plain integer if `m + n` is odd, otherwise print it with **exactly one decimal place** (e.g. `3.0`, `2.5`).

Given an array of integers, sort it in **ascending order** using **Patience Sort** and print the sorted array.

Print space-separated integers on one line.
Given a 32-bit unsigned integer `n`, print the integer obtained by **reversing its bits**.

Given an `m x n` matrix where each row is sorted ascending and each row's first integer is greater than the previous row's last integer, print `true` if `target` exists in the matrix, else `false`. Solve in **O(log(m*n))** time by treating the matrix as one flattened sorted array.
Given an integer array `nums` and window size `k`, print the **maximum value** of every contiguous window of size `k` as it slides from left to right, space-separated in order.
Given a string `s` and an integer `k`, print the number of **distinct substrings of length exactly `k`**. Solve using a rolling hash to compare substrings in O(1) after O(n) preprocessing.
Given a string `s` and a dictionary of words `wordDict` (one line, space separated), print the number of **distinct ways** to segment `s` into a space-separated sequence of dictionary words.
